## reference · answer engine optimization
* Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is the practice of optimizing content to be cited by AI-powered search platforms.
* AEO is a component of Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), focusing on the answer-retrieval layer.
* AI search engines use Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to find, evaluate, and synthesize content.
* AEO requires content to be structured for AI parsing, with clear headings, semantic chunking, and logical structure.
* Answer engines prioritize sources with:
  + Clear, direct answers to specific questions
  + Statistics and data points with cited sources
  + Logical structure with headings and semantic chunking
  + Authoritative signals (backlinks, domain reputation, E-E-A-T)
  + Content freshness and recency
  + Schema markup and structured data
* AEO differs from traditional SEO in:
  + Primary goal (get cited in AI-generated answers vs. rank on SERPs)
  + Success metric (AI citations, brand mentions, referral traffic from AI vs. rankings, organic traffic, CTR)
  + Optimization unit (fact-level vs. page-level)
  + Content structure (semantically chunked, extractable sections vs. comprehensive, long-form coverage)
* Both AEO and traditional SEO reward high-quality, well-structured content and value E-E-A-T signals.
* AEO requires an additional layer of optimization: making every section independently understandable and every key fact independently citable.
* AI search adoption is accelerating, with AI referral traffic accounting for 1.08% of total website traffic and growing at approximately 1% monthly.
* ChatGPT alone handles over 2 billion queries daily, and AI-referred sessions to websites grew 527% year-over-year through mid-2025.
* Google AI Overviews appear in nearly 55% of all Google searches, and Gartner predicts traditional search volume will drop 25% by 2026 due to AI chatbots and virtual agents.
Sources: https://www.frase.io/blog/what-is-answer-engine-optimization-the-complete-guide-to-getting-cited-by-ai
